title: Tall Nettles by Edward Thomas — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
TALL nettles cover up, as they have done
These many springs, the rusty harrow, the plough
Long worn out, and the roller made of stone:
Only the elm butt tops the nettles now.

This corner of the farmyard I like most:
As well as any bloom upon a flower
I like the dust on the nettles, never lost
Except to prove the sweetness of a shower.
meaning: "Tall Nettles" by Edward Thomas is a nature english poem consisting of 9 lines. This English poem by Edward Thomas demonstrates the timeless power of verse to capture complex human emotions. Beginning with "TALL nettles cover up, as they have done These many springs, the rusty harrow, the plough...", this piece explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional resonance. The work invites contemplation on the deeper currents of life, love, and the human condition. Edward Thomas's celebrated body of poetry continues to inspire readers across generations and cultures, and this particular work stands as a powerful example of their artistic vision.
